title: TealTiger TealEngine Policy
description: JSON Schema for TealEngine policy configuration files. It covers the TealPolicy interface used by TealTiger's deterministic policy engine.

$schema.description: Optional schema URI used by editors to provide autocomplete and validation for this policy file.

tools.description: Tool usage policies keyed by tool name. Each entry controls whether a tool is allowed and which limits apply to its use.

identity.description: Agent identity and role-based access control policy.

codeExecution.description: Code generation and execution restrictions for agents that can produce or run code.

behavioral.description: Behavioral monitoring limits, including cost ceilings, request rate limits, and anomaly thresholds.

memory.description: Memory and context storage policy for agent memory operations.

content.description: Content filtering and moderation policy, including PII detection and safety category thresholds.

toolPolicy.description: Map of tool names to per-tool policy configuration. Use '*' to define a wildcard policy.

toolConfig.description: Policy configuration for a single tool.

allowed.description: Whether the tool is allowed to be used.

maxSize.description: Maximum data size for tool parameters or operations, such as '1MB', '500KB', or '10GB'.

rateLimit.description: Maximum tool calls allowed during a time window.

allowedTables.description: Database table names the tool may access when the tool performs database operations.

maxRows.description: Maximum number of rows a database tool may return.

parameters.description: Additional tool-specific parameters. TealEngine treats these as opaque policy metadata for integrations.

max.description: Maximum number of calls allowed during the configured window.

window.description: Time window for the limit, such as '1m', '1h', or '1d'.

identityPolicy.description: Identity and role-based access control policy for a single agent.

agentId.description: Unique agent identifier expected in evaluated requests.

permissions.description: Allowed permissions for the agent. Permissions commonly use 'action:resource' format.

items.description: Permission string granted to the agent.

forbidden.description: Actions or wildcard patterns that are explicitly forbidden for this identity.

items.description: Forbidden action or wildcard pattern, such as 'delete:*'.

costLimit.description: Optional cost limits scoped to this identity.

codeExecutionPolicy.description: Policy for generated or executable code.

allowedLanguages.description: Programming languages the agent may generate or execute. Use '*' only for permissive development policies.

blockedFunctions.description: Function names that generated or executed code must not contain, such as 'eval', 'exec', or 'system'.

blockedPatterns.description: Regular expression patterns that generated or executed code must not match. JSON and YAML policies should store these as pattern strings.

requireSandbox.description: Whether code execution requires a sandbox.

sandboxRequired.description: Deprecated alias for requireSandbox retained for compatibility with older policy examples.

behavioralPolicy.description: Runtime behavior limits for an agent.

costLimit.description: Cost ceilings for requests evaluated under this policy.

rateLimit.description: Request rate limit for the agent or policy.

anomalyThreshold.description: Anomaly detection threshold multiplier. For example, 2.0 means 200% of baseline.

memoryPolicy.description: Policy for data stored in agent memory or context.

validateInputs.description: Whether inputs should be validated before they are stored in memory.

sanitizeBeforeStorage.description: Whether content should be sanitized before it is stored in memory.

blockedPatterns.description: Regular expression patterns for content that must not be stored in memory. JSON and YAML policies should store these as pattern strings.

contentPolicy.description: Policy for content filtering, PII handling, and moderation.

blockedTypes.description: PII types to block or redact, such as 'ssn', 'credit_card', 'email', 'phone', or 'address'.

redactInLogs.description: Whether detected PII should be redacted in logs.

categories.description: Moderation categories to evaluate, such as 'hate', 'violence', 'sexual', or 'self-harm'.

requests.description: Maximum number of requests allowed during the configured window.

window.description: Time window for the request limit, such as '1m', '1h', or '1d'.

timeWindow.description: Duration string with a number and unit: seconds (s), minutes (m), hours (h), or days (d).
